# LTXV-2 Model Files & Dependencies

## Diffusion Model
**LTXV-2.3-22b DISTILLED FP8**  
*Place in:* `unet`

- [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/diffusion_models/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled_transformer_only_fp8_input_scaled.safetensors)

---

## Text Encoder
**Gemma-3-12b-it-IQ4_XS.gguf**  
*Place in:* `text_encoders`

- [gemma-3-12b-it-IQ4_XS.gguf](https://huggingface.co/unsloth/gemma-3-12b-it-GGUF/resolve/main/gemma-3-12b-it-IQ4_XS.gguf?download=true)

---

A wide-angle cinematic shot captures the sprawling Bremer farm at daybreak. Robert, a weathered man in his late 30s, works near a tractor, his 7-year-old son Lucas "helping" beside him. Elena, wearing a practical apron, sweeps the porch with baby Anni strapped to her back. Their teenage children, Chris (15) and Emma (14), are subtly working on chores, while the younger siblings (Lea, 10, Sara, 6, Rebecca, 4) occupy themselves with play in the misty morning light. The scene is calm, almost idyllic, showcasing a family bound by the rhythm of rural life. The composition emphasizes the vastness of the landscape and the smallness of the family within it. Shot on a Canon EOS C700, 35mm lens, natural film grain.

Prompt (German - Dialogue, but for scene description in film generation, use the above):

Robert Bremer (38) and his wife Elena (39) were surprised to find the earth in ruins from nuclear war. But Robert had a secret: a spaceship hidden in Earth's orbit. As the situation grew direr, he gathered his family on their Nebraska farm, and they vanished, leaving their ruined home behind. Safe aboard the ship, they now faced a monumental task: finding a way to bring peace back to a world consumed by violence. "Wir müssen einen Weg finden, dies zu beenden," Robert said, gazing down at the battered planet. "Es gibt immer Hoffnung," Elena replied, squeezing his hand. They began their journey, determined to save the world they called home.

inputs.text: A medium close-up shot features a Caucasian man with a closely shaven head and face, wearing a black baseball cap with "PNTR" in white letters on the front, and a dark grey t-shirt with "JUST DO IT" visible across his chest. A small black microphone is clipped to his shirt collar. He is positioned slightly to the left of the frame, looking intently downwards and to his right, his eyes focused off-camera. His facial expression is one of deep concentration, with his brow slightly furrowed. As he looks down, a quick sniff sound is heard, and then he speaks with a deep male voice and a slightly frustrated tone, saying, "I think it's so bad." The camera remains static throughout, maintaining a shallow depth of field, which keeps the man in sharp focus while the background is softly blurred, revealing a light-colored wall with white wooden shelving or trim, and a partially open white wooden door on the right. After a brief pause, another short, audible sniff is heard. The man then continues to speak, his voice maintaining the same quality, as he states, "So bad. So bad." He elaborates further, emphasizing his point with a final statement, "This got to be, it's got to be the worst tool I've ever seen."
blurry, low quality, still frame, frames, watermark, overlay, titles, has blurbox, has subtitles
